chapter_title.en: The Prayer of Fear, Pursuit, Encounter, and Competition

text: 1348 - وَ فِي كِتَابِ عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ بْنِ اَلْمُغِيرَةِ أَنَّ اَلصَّادِقَ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ: «أَقَلُّ مَا يُجْزِي فِي حَدِّ اَلْمُسَايَفَةِ مِنَ اَلتَّكْبِيرِ تَكْبِيرَتَانِ لِكُلِّ صَلاَةٍ إِلاَّ اَلْمَغْرِبَ فَإِنَّ لَهَا ثَلاَثاً مِنَ اَلتَّكْبِيرِ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.1348 - In the book of Abdullah ibn al-Mughira, it is reported that Al-Sadiq (as) said:
"The minimum that suffices for the prayer during close combat (al-musayafa) is two takbirs (proclamations of 'Allahu Akbar') for each prayer, except for the Maghrib prayer, for which there are three takbirs."
